methods.method_name: Biomacromolecule Encapsulation by Vacuum Loading Technique
methods.description: This method describes the process of loading biomacromolecules into SECs using a vacuum loading technique to ensure efficient encapsulation. The method is used to prepare SECs for further analysis and characterization.

steps.description: 150 mg of untreated spores were suspended in 0.6 ml of 125 mg/ml aqueous BSA solution, and 150 mg SECs were suspended in 1.2 ml of 125 mg/ml aqueous BSA solution in polypropylene tubes to form a homogeneous suspension and then mixed for 10 min using a vortex mixer.
steps.searchable_summary: suspend spores and SECs in BSA solution and mix
outputs: homogeneous suspension of spores and SECs

steps.description: The tubes were transferred to a freeze dryer and a vacuum was applied at 1 mbar for 2 h.
steps.searchable_summary: apply vacuum to suspension in freeze dryer

steps.description: The tubes were collected and the loaded SECs were washed using 2 ml water then centrifuged to remove residual BSA, this washing step was repeated for a total of two washes.
steps.searchable_summary: wash and centrifuge SECs to remove residual BSA

steps.description: The SECs were then placed in a freezer at −70 °C for 30 min before freeze-drying for 24 h.
The resultant SECs were stored at −20 °C until characterization.
Placebo capsules were prepared with the same procedure, except BSA, and also stored at −20 °C.
FITC-conjugated BSA was encapsulated in SECs using the same procedure as the above-mentioned vacuum loading technique.

methods.description: This method measures the efficiency of BSA encapsulation in SECs by extracting and quantifying the BSA content using a standard curve.

steps.description: 5 mg of BSA-loaded SECs were suspended in 1.4 mL of PBS, then vortexed for 5 min, and probe sonicated for 3 cycles of 10 s (40% amplitude).
steps.searchable_summary: suspend and sonicate BSA-loaded SECs in PBS

steps.description: The solution was filtered to collect the extracted BSA using a 0.45 μm PES syringe filter.
The absorbance values were measured at 280 nm using a placebo extract as a blank and the amount of BSA in the SECs was calculated using a BSA standard curve.
steps.searchable_summary: measure absorbance and calculate BSA amount

methods.description: This method involves performing statistical analysis on the quantitative data obtained from the experiments to determine significance.

steps.description: Statistical analysis was performed using two-tailed t-tests and P < 0.05 was considered as statistically significant.
Quantitative data are reported as mean values ± standard deviation of three separate measurements.
